title: IBS symptom review
source_review_basis: full authoritative documents inspected and archived; item-level retain/rewrite/remove/add comparisons recorded

assessment.text: Supports agreed symptom-response-based follow-up, annual review, and investigation or referral when new red-flag symptoms emerge.

source.title: Irritable bowel syndrome in adults: diagnosis and management
source.population: Adults with suspected or confirmed irritable bowel syndrome.
source.setting: Primary-care assessment, investigation, management, and follow-up.
source.exclusions: Exact for selected adult IBS symptom-profile, investigation, lifestyle-discussion, and follow-up fields; the legacy workflow does not enforce adult age or the required symptom duration and diagnostic criteria.
source.evidence_paraphrase: Supports at least 6 months of abdominal pain or discomfort, bloating, bowel-habit change, relation to defaecation or stool frequency or form, straining, urgency, incomplete evacuation, food association, mucus, and selected extra-intestinal symptoms.

assessment.text: Supports agreed symptom-response-based follow-up, annual review, and investigation or referral when new red-flag symptoms emerge.

assessment.text: Supports at least 6 months of abdominal pain or discomfort, bloating, bowel-habit change, relation to defaecation or stool frequency or form, straining, urgency, incomplete evacuation, food association, mucus, and selected extra-intestinal symptoms.
assessment.rationale: The exact adult section supports interval abdominal pain, bloating, relation to defaecation, bowel frequency or form, urgency, incomplete evacuation, food association, mucus, duration, and impact without independently diagnosing IBS.

source.title: Irritable bowel syndrome in adults: diagnosis and management
source.population: Adults with suspected or confirmed irritable bowel syndrome.

source.exclusions: Exact for selected adult IBS symptom-profile, investigation, lifestyle-discussion, and follow-up fields; the legacy workflow does not enforce adult age or the required symptom duration and diagnostic criteria.
source.evidence_paraphrase: Supports at least 6 months of abdominal pain or discomfort, bloating, bowel-habit change, relation to defaecation or stool frequency or form, straining, urgency, incomplete evacuation, food association, mucus, and selected extra-intestinal symptoms.

assessment.text: Requires clinical assessment and examination for suspected-cancer features and inflammatory markers for inflammatory bowel disease, with referral when present; specific cancer-feature fields require their own exact source.
assessment.rationale: The exact red-flag section supports clinician assessment and concern documentation without asserting absence.

source.title: Irritable bowel syndrome in adults: diagnosis and management
source.population: Adults with suspected or confirmed irritable bowel syndrome.
source.setting: Primary-care assessment, investigation, management, and follow-up.
source.exclusions: Exact for selected adult IBS symptom-profile, investigation, lifestyle-discussion, and follow-up fields; the legacy workflow does not enforce adult age or the required symptom duration and diagnostic criteria.
source.evidence_paraphrase: Requires clinical assessment and examination for suspected-cancer features and inflammatory markers for inflammatory bowel disease, with referral when present; specific cancer-feature fields require their own exact source.

assessment.text: Supports full blood count, ESR or plasma viscosity, CRP, and coeliac antibodies in people meeting IBS criteria and identifies tests not required solely to confirm IBS.
assessment.rationale: The exact test section supports clinician review of already available blood and coeliac results in appropriately assessed adults.
